value.body:
Found during a direct Node API audit of runtime source (`packages/**/src`, excluding tests, scripts, vendor, and generated protocol files).

Problem:
API runtime services still read process globals and global crypto directly, even where `NodeServices.layer` and Effect `Crypto` are available.

Evidence:
- `packages/api/src/server.ts:104` writes the runtime discovery file with `globalThis.process?.pid`.
- `packages/api/src/server.ts:136-146` defaults hosted MCP env by spreading `process.env`.
- `packages/api/src/CycleApi.ts:180-185` defaults MCP env to `process.env`.
- `packages/api/src/http/handlers/v1/chat/ws.ts:1751` creates chat IDs with global `crypto.randomUUID()`.
- `packages/api/src/agents/services/AgentActiveTurnDirectory.ts:52-61` creates turn IDs with global `crypto.randomUUID()`.
- `packages/api/src/agents/services/AgentProviderProfiles.ts:19-24` calls agent provider detection with `process.env`.

Suggested remediation:
- Add/provide a small API runtime env/process service, or require env/pid as part of server/API options.
- Use `Crypto.Crypto` for chat IDs and active turn IDs.
- Thread env into agent provider profile detection rather than reading process globals in the service.

Acceptance criteria:
- API package runtime source no longer reads `process.env`, `globalThis.process`, or global `crypto` outside intentional Node entrypoints/platform adapters.
- Runtime discovery output still includes a pid when provided by the Node runtime layer.
- Existing hosted MCP and agent provider behavior remains unchanged for desktop and CLI callers.

frontmatter.title: Remove direct process and crypto reads from API runtime services

value.title: Remove direct process and crypto reads from API runtime services
